title: Codex now offers more flexible pricing for teams
optimized_headline: Codex Introduces Flexible Team Pricing: What’s Changed?
raw_excerpt: Codex now includes pay-as-you-go pricing for ChatGPT Business and Enterprise, providing teams a more flexible option to start and scale adoption.
raw_body: Codex now includes pay-as-you-go pricing for ChatGPT Business and Enterprise, providing teams a more flexible option to start and scale adoption.
speedrun: Codex has introduced a pay-as-you-go pricing model for its ChatGPT Business and Enterprise offerings. This change allows teams to adopt and scale the service more flexibly, catering to different usage needs. With this model, organizations can better manage costs while exploring the capabilities of AI. This shift is significant as it could lower the barrier for teams to integrate AI into their workflows.
why_it_matters: Teams can now adopt AI tools without committing to large upfront costs, making it easier for them to experiment with AI solutions.
This pricing model reflects a broader trend in the market towards more adaptable and user-friendly solutions, encouraging wider AI adoption across industries.

lenses.eli12: Codex has made it easier for teams to use AI by offering a pay-as-you-go pricing plan. Think of it like paying for a streaming service only when you watch shows. This flexibility could help more people and businesses use AI without worrying about hefty fees.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, this new pricing model addresses a key user need for flexibility and cost management. It allows teams to experiment with the AI tools without significant upfront investment, which could lead to better product iterations and user feedback. This could enhance overall efficiency in adopting AI solutions.
lenses.engineer: From a technical perspective, the introduction of pay-as-you-go pricing for ChatGPT Business and Enterprise could facilitate more dynamic usage patterns. Teams can now scale their AI usage based on real-time needs, optimizing resource allocation. This flexibility might lead to increased experimentation with AI features, ultimately driving innovation.
